# react-css-marquee

> Lightweight, zero-dependency React marquee component powered by CSS animations.

Install: `npm install react-css-marquee`  
Import: `import { Marquee } from 'react-css-marquee'`  
Peer deps: React ≥ 16.8

## What it does

Renders children inside a continuously scrolling track. By default children are duplicated enough times to fill the viewport for a seamless infinite loop (`repeat={true}`). Setting `repeat={false}` produces a "breaking news" ticker where each item scrolls across once and stops.

Uses a single CSS `@keyframes` animation injected via an inline `<style>` tag — no JS animation loop, no requestAnimationFrame, no external CSS file needed.

## Key API

| Prop | Type | Default | Notes |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| `children` | `ReactNode` | — | Content to scroll |
| `direction` | `'horizontal' \| 'vertical'` | `'horizontal'` | Vertical marquees need a fixed height via `style` |
| `speed` | `number` | `10` | CSS `animation-duration` in seconds — **higher = slower** |
| `gap` | `number` | `40` | Gap between items in pixels |
| `repeat` | `boolean` | `true` | `false` = ticker mode, items scroll across once |
| `reverse` | `boolean` | `false` | Flip scroll direction |
| `loop` | `number` | `0` | Cycle count; `0` = infinite |
| `delay` | `number` | `0` | Seconds before animation starts |
| `gradient` | `boolean` | `false` | Fade edges with CSS `mask-image` (works over any background) |
| `gradientWidth` | `number \| string` | `200` | Fade zone width; number = px, string = any CSS unit |
| `isPaused` | `boolean` | `false` | Pause programmatically |
| `isPausedOnHover` | `boolean` | `false` | Pause on pointer hover (mouse + touch) |
| `isPausedOnClick` | `boolean` | `false` | Click to toggle pause |
| `isPausedOnMouseDown` | `boolean` | `false` | Pause while mouse/touch held down |
| `draggable` | `boolean` | `false` | Drag to scrub; animation resumes from dragged position |
| `onCycleComplete` | `() => void` | — | Fired on each completed cycle |
| `onFinish` | `() => void` | — | Fired when a finite `loop` finishes |
| `className` | `string` | — | CSS class for outer container |
| `style` | `CSSProperties` | — | Inline styles for outer container |

## Usage examples

### Basic logo strip
```tsx
<Marquee gap={32} speed={15}>
  <img src="logo-a.svg" />
  <img src="logo-b.svg" />
  <img src="logo-c.svg" />
</Marquee>
```

### Vertical feed, gradient edges, pause on hover
```tsx
<Marquee direction="vertical" gradient gradientWidth={80} isPausedOnHover style={{ height: 400 }}>
  <Card title="Item 1" />
  <Card title="Item 2" />
</Marquee>
```

### Ticker — scroll once, no repeat
```tsx
<Marquee repeat={false} speed={8}>
  <h2>Breaking: major update shipped</h2>
</Marquee>
```

### Reverse direction
```tsx
<Marquee reverse speed={12}>
  <span>Scrolls right-to-left... wait, left-to-right</span>
</Marquee>
```

### Finite loop with callback
```tsx
<Marquee loop={3} onFinish={() => setDone(true)}>
  <span>Plays exactly three times</span>
</Marquee>
```

### Draggable photo strip
```tsx
<Marquee draggable gap={16}>
  <img src="photo1.jpg" />
  <img src="photo2.jpg" />
</Marquee>
```

### Pause from outside the component
```tsx
const [paused, setPaused] = useState(false);
<button onClick={() => setPaused(p => !p)}>Toggle</button>
<Marquee isPaused={paused}>
  <span>Controlled pause</span>
</Marquee>
```

## Accessibility

- Automatically pauses when `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is set.
- Duplicate copies rendered for seamless looping are marked `aria-hidden` so screen readers only see the content once.

## Notes for code generation

- Always wrap children in the `<Marquee>` component — do **not** try to recreate the animation with custom CSS.
- For vertical marquees, `style={{ height: '...' }}` is required on the component or a parent; it does not have an implicit height.
- `speed` is a **duration**, not a velocity — doubling the value halves the speed.
- `loop={0}` (the default) means infinite; any positive integer is an exact cycle count.
- `gradient` uses `mask-image`, not a colour overlay, so it works on any background without knowing the background colour.
- The component is SSR-safe; it measures itself via `ResizeObserver` after mount.
